source_paper_title: A Unified Analysis of a Class of Proximal Bundle Methods for Solving Hybrid Convex Composite Optimization Problems

C.}, year={2024}, month=may, pages={832–855} }
context:
Section 6 (Concluding Remarks), page 21 (paragraph beginning 'First, under the assumption that the diameter D of dom h is finite, ...').

It would be interesting to design an accelerated variant of GPB which is optimal for the aforementioned HCCO problem class.
title: Design accelerated proximal bundle methods for hybrid convex composite optimization problems
background_latex:
Let \(f,h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) be proper lower semicontinuous convex functions with \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\subseteq \mathrm{dom}\,f\). Consider the hybrid convex composite optimization problem
\[
\phi^*:=\min\{\phi(x):=f(x)+h(x): x\in\mathbb{R}^n\}.
\]
Assume \(h\) is \(\mu\)-convex for some \(\mu\ge 0\), meaning that \(h(x)-\frac{\mu}{2}\|x\|^2\) is convex. Assume a first-order oracle \(f'\) is available with \(f'(x)\in \partial f(x)\) for all \(x\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\), and there exist parameters \(M_f\ge 0\) and \(L_f\ge 0\) such that for all \(u,v\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\),
\[
\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f + L_f\|u-v\|.
\]
Let \(D\) denote the diameter of \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) when it is finite:
\[
D:=\sup\{\|x-y\|: x,y\in \mathrm{dom}\,h\}<\infty.
\]
An \(\bar\varepsilon\)-solution is a point \(\bar x\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\) with \(\phi(\bar x)-\phi^*\le \bar\varepsilon\).

A proximal bundle (PB) method is a first-order method that iteratively solves proximalized subproblems based on a piecewise-linear (bundle) lower model of \(f\) plus \(h\), typically producing serious/null steps; the paper analyzes a generic PB framework (GPB) and obtains iteration-complexity bounds of order
\[
O\!\left(\min\left\{\frac{(M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f)d_0^2}{\bar\varepsilon^2},\;\left(\frac{M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f}{\mu\bar\varepsilon}+1\right)\log\left(\frac{\mu d_0^2}{\bar\varepsilon}+1\right)\right\}+1\right)
\]
(up to logarithmic factors), where \(d_0\) is the distance from the initial point to the solution set.

For the same HCCO class with finite \(D\), known lower bounds (cited in the paper) imply that an optimal (accelerated) rate in terms of \((L_f,M_f,D,\bar\varepsilon)\) is
\[
O\!\left( \frac{\sqrt{L_f}\,D}{\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}} + \frac{M_f^2 D^2}{\bar\varepsilon^2}\right)
\]
(in the sense described in the cited reference).
problem_definition_latex:
Assume \(D:=\sup\{\|x-y\|:x,y\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\}<\infty\). Develop a (proximal) bundle-type method for minimizing \(\phi(x)=f(x)+h(x)\) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\) whose iteration complexity to obtain an \(\bar\varepsilon\)-solution matches (up to universal constants and allowable logarithmic factors) the optimal bound
\[
O\!\left( \frac{\sqrt{L_f}\,D}{\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}} + \frac{M_f^2 D^2}{\bar\varepsilon^2}\right).
\]
Equivalently, design an accelerated variant of the generic proximal bundle framework achieving the above complexity for the HCCO problem class parameterized by \((L_f,M_f,D)\).

location_in_paper: Section 6 (Concluding Remarks), page 21 (paragraph beginning 'First, under the assumption that the diameter D of dom h is finite, ...').
open_problem_quote: It would be interesting to design an accelerated variant of GPB which is optimal for the aforementioned HCCO problem class.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for designing an algorithm with a provable worst-case complexity guarantee, not for refuting a conjectured inequality. Automated instance search is unlikely to yield decisive counterexamples because the claim concerns universal worst-case rates over a broad function class.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: There is a searchable design space (bundle update rules, momentum/estimate-sequence parameters, adaptive prox stepsizes) and a computable proxy fitness signal via empirical performance on representative synthetic HCCO instances. However, the target is a worst-case theoretical rate, and empirical optimization may not translate into provable acceleration without substantial analytic insight.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In Liang--Monteiro's HCCO model, the oracle condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\) interpolates between Lipschitz nonsmoothness (\(L_f=0\)) and smoothness (\(M_f=0\)). Their GPB framework (with serious/null steps and bundle models \(\Gamma\le \phi\)) yields a unified complexity of order \(O((M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f)d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) in the convex case \(\mu=0\), and a logarithmic dependence in \(1/\bar\varepsilon\) when \(\mu>0\). The paper explicitly notes that, when \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) has finite diameter \(D\), the optimal worst-case bound should be \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\), matching accelerated composite subgradient methods, and poses the open problem of designing an accelerated bundle-type analogue.

Forward citations show partial progress but no full resolution. The closest acceleration result is "On the Acceleration of Proximal Bundle Methods", which develops accelerated PB variants and proves \(\tilde O(\sqrt{L_f}\,\|x_0-x^*\|/\sqrt{\varepsilon})\) iteration complexity (up to \(\log(1/\varepsilon)\)) in the smooth regime \(M_f=0\), aligning with the \(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\varepsilon}\) term when \(D\) controls distances. However, it does not cover the hybrid regime \(M_f>0\) nor establish the mixed bound combining \(\varepsilon^{-1/2}\) and \(\varepsilon^{-2}\) terms for general HCCO with prox-friendly \(h\). Other works (e.g., universal/adaptive GPB variants and primal-dual PB analyses) strengthen universality, adaptivity, and primal-dual certification, and sharpen strongly convex rates, but their convex-case dependence on \(L_f\) remains non-accelerated (typically \(L_f/\varepsilon\) rather than \(\sqrt{L_f/\varepsilon}\)).

Overall, the evidence suggests the specific open problem remains open: there is not yet a proximal bundle-type method for general convex HCCO with bounded \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) that provably matches \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) (up to logs). Promising directions indicated by the citations include combining Nesterov-style acceleration of inexact proximal point/bundle subproblem solves with hybrid-model-aware step selection, and leveraging primal-dual viewpoints (e.g., PDCP--conditional-gradient duality) to control null steps without introducing geometry-dependent constants.

sources.title: On the Acceleration of Proximal Bundle Methods
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper proposes accelerated proximal bundle variants and proves accelerated iteration complexity in two settings. For unconstrained smooth convex minimization \(\min_x f(x)\) with \(\nabla f\) \(L_f\)-Lipschitz, Algorithm 3 (a-PBM) reaches an \(\varepsilon\)-optimal point in \(O\!\left(\sqrt{\tfrac{\rho\,\|x_0-x^*\|^2}{\varepsilon}}\,\log\tfrac1\varepsilon\right)\) iterations (Theorem 26; e.g. choosing \(\rho=L_f\) gives \(O(\sqrt{L_f}\,\|x_0-x^*\|/\sqrt{\varepsilon}\cdot\log(1/\varepsilon))\)). For composite objectives \(h(x)=g(x)+f(x)\) with \(g\) smooth and \(f\) convex piecewise-linear, Algorithm 5 attains \(O\!\left(\sqrt{\tfrac{1}{\varepsilon}}\,\log\tfrac1\varepsilon\right)\) serious-step complexity and an overall bound of the form \(O\!\left(\sqrt{\tfrac{1}{\varepsilon}}\,\log\tfrac1\varepsilon\right)\) times a dimension/geometric factor controlling null steps (Theorem 37).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle-type method for the hybrid convex composite problem \(\min_x f(x)+h(x)\) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\) with finite domain diameter \(D\), achieving the optimal complexity \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) (up to logs). This paper makes clear progress on the \(M_f=0\) (smooth) regime by giving the first accelerated PBM variant with \(O(\sqrt{L_f}/\sqrt{\varepsilon}\cdot\log(1/\varepsilon))\) dependence (matching the \(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\varepsilon}\) term up to replacing \(D\) by \(\|x_0-x^*\|\) and a log factor). However, it does not address the full hybrid condition with \(M_f>0\) nor provide the mixed-rate bound \(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\varepsilon^2\) for general HCCO with a general nonsmooth \(h\) (prox-friendly) and bounded \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\). The composite result treated here is different (smooth + piecewise-linear) and its complexity depends on additional geometric quantities (e.g., pyramidal width) rather than the \((M_f,L_f,D)\) parameters of the open problem.

sources.title: Universal subgradient and proximal bundle methods for convex and strongly convex hybrid composite optimization
sources.bibtex:
@article{UniversalSubgradientAndProximalBundleMethods2025,
  title   = {Universal subgradient and proximal bundle methods for convex and strongly convex hybrid composite optimization},
  author  = {},
  journal = {Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications},
  year    = {2025},
  doi     = {10.1007/s10957-025-02927-7},
}
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proposes a parameter-free (universal) proximal bundle method U-PB and proves functional iteration complexity bounds for hybrid composite optimization \(\min_x\,\phi(x)=f(x)+h(x)\) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(x)-f'(y)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|x-y\|\). Under bounded \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) with diameter \(D\), Theorem 2.3 bounds the total number of inner (bundle) iterations by
- \(\min\{\min[\tfrac1\chi(N+U(\bar\varepsilon)/(\bar\varepsilon\mu_\phi)),\; N+U(\bar\varepsilon)/(\bar\varepsilon\mu_h)]\,\log(1+\mu_\phi d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon),\; d_0^2 U(\bar\varepsilon)/(\bar\varepsilon^2 N)\}+ N\lceil 2\log(\lambda_0 U(\bar\varepsilon)/(N\bar\varepsilon))\rceil,\)
where \(U(\bar\varepsilon)=\tfrac{4(M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f)B}{(1-\chi)^2}+\tfrac{N\bar\varepsilon}{\lambda_0}\) and \(B=8+12\log(1+L_f^2D^2N/(16M_f^2))\). In particular, in the strongly convex regime \(\mu_\phi>0\) and with \(\chi\) bounded away from \(0,1\), this yields \(\tilde O(M_f^2/(\bar\varepsilon\mu_\phi)+L_f/\mu_\phi)\) dependence (up to logs).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for an accelerated bundle-type method for the same HCCO class (bounded \(D\)) achieving the optimal convex (\(\mu=0\)) rate \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\). This paper develops a universal proximal bundle method (U-PB) and sharpens GPB-type complexity in terms of strong convexity \(\mu_\phi\), without restarts or knowing parameters. However, its main bounds are either (i) strongly convex/\(\mu_\phi\)-universal rates of order \(\tilde O(M_f^2/(\bar\varepsilon\mu_\phi)+L_f/\mu_\phi)\), or (ii) a non-strongly-convex fallback term \(O(d_0^2(M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f)/\bar\varepsilon^2)\). It does not provide an accelerated \(\mu=0\) bundle method matching the optimal \(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\) term; the authors explicitly note acceleration as future work. Thus it is relevant progress on universal PB design and analysis, but it does not solve the stated acceleration open problem.

sources.title: Parameter-free proximal bundle methods with adaptive stepsizes for hybrid convex composite optimization problems
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proposes Ad-GPB, a parameter-free adaptive modern proximal bundle framework for HCCO with bounded \,\(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) (diameter \(D\)) and hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M+L\|u-v\|\). Its main guarantee (Theorem 2.1) is that Ad-GPB outputs \(\hat y_k\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\) with \(\phi(\hat y_k)-\phi^*\le \phi(\hat y_k)-\hat n_k\le \varepsilon\) within at most \(4\bar K(\varepsilon)\) cycles and at most
\[4\bar K(\varepsilon)\Big(1+\tfrac{\tau}{1-\tau}\log_+\tfrac{8\bar t}{\varepsilon}+2\Big)+\log_+^2\Big(\bar Q\lambda_1\big(\tfrac{M^2}{\varepsilon}+\tfrac{L}{16}\big)\Big)\]
iterations, where \(\bar t=2MD+\tfrac{L}{2}D^2\), \(\bar Q=128(1-\tau)/\tau\), and \(\bar K(\varepsilon)=2\left\lceil \tfrac{2D^2\bar Q}{\varepsilon}\left(\tfrac{M^2}{\varepsilon}+\tfrac{L}{16}+\tfrac{1}{\lambda_1\bar Q}\right)+\log_+\tfrac{\beta_0(\phi(\hat x_0)-\hat n_0)}{\varepsilon}+1\right\rceil\).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for an accelerated (optimal-rate) proximal bundle-type method for HCCO with bounded \(D\) achieving complexity \(\tilde O(\sqrt{L}\,D/\sqrt{\varepsilon}+M^2D^2/\varepsilon^2)\). This paper studies adaptive/parameter-free step-size and cycle-termination rules for modern PB methods under the same hybrid condition and bounded \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\). However, its proven iteration complexity remains essentially \(\tilde O\big(D^2(M^2/\varepsilon^2+L/\varepsilon)\big)\) (plus logs and dependence on \(\lambda_1\)), i.e., it does not achieve the accelerated \(\sqrt{L}/\sqrt{\varepsilon}\) dependence. Thus it does not solve the open problem, but provides partial progress in the direction of practical adaptivity and parameter-free tuning within the proximal bundle framework for the same problem class.

sources.title: Primal-dual proximal bundle and conditional gradient methods for convex problems: J. Liang
sources.bibtex:
@article{PrimalDualProximalBundleAndConditionalGradientMethods2025,
  title   = {Primal-dual proximal bundle and conditional gradient methods for convex problems: J. Liang},
  author  = {},
  journal = {Mathematical Programming},
  year    = {2025},
  doi     = {10.1007/s10107-025-02307-z},
}
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops a primal-dual proximal bundle framework (PDPB) for convex nonsmooth composite optimization 
\(\min_x\,\phi(x)=f(x)+h(x)\) under the bounded-subgradient assumption \(\|f'(x)\|\le M\) on \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\). For the PDCP inner cutting-plane scheme with \(\tau_j=j/(j+2)\), it proves a cycle complexity bound: PDCP reaches primal-dual gap \(t_j\le\varepsilon\) in 
- \(O\big(\sqrt{t_1/\varepsilon}+\lambda M^2/\varepsilon+1\big)\) inner iterations (Proposition 2.3),
and the outer PDPB method achieves total oracle complexity 
\[O\big(M^2 d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon^2+1\big)\]
for producing \((\bar x_k,\bar s_k)\) with primal-dual gap \(\hat\phi(\bar x_k)+f^*(\bar s_k)+\hat h^*(-\bar s_k)\le 10\bar\varepsilon\) (Theorem 2.1), where \(\hat h=h+I_Q\) for a ball \(Q\) around the start point.
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle-type method for the hybrid convex composite class with the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\) and bounded domain diameter \(D\), achieving the optimal complexity \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) (up to logs).

This citing paper studies proximal bundle methods for the same composite form \(f+h\) but assumes only uniformly bounded subgradients \(\|f'(x)\|\le M\) (assumption (A2)), i.e., it corresponds essentially to the special case \(L_f=0\) and \(M_f\asymp M\) (no Lipschitz-gradient component exploited). Its main complexity for CNCO is \(O(M^2 d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\), matching the \(M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2\) term but it does not obtain the accelerated \(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\) term when \(L_f>0\). The paper’s contributions are therefore partial progress: it strengthens guarantees to a primal-dual gap and develops primal-dual/CG duality tools, but it does not design an accelerated PB method achieving the full optimal bound for general \((L_f,M_f,D)\).

sources.title: Proximal oracles for optimization and sampling
sources.bibtex:
@article{ProximalOraclesForOptimizationAndSampling2026,
  title   = {Proximal oracles for optimization and sampling},
  author  = {},
  journal = {Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications},
  year    = {2026},
  doi     = {10.1007/s10957-026-02937-z},
}
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper analyzes a regularized cutting-plane (proximal bundle) procedure for approximately solving proximal subproblems \(\min_x f(x)+\tfrac{1}{2\eta}\|x-y\|^2\) when \(f\) is Hölder-smooth or a hybrid sum of Hölder-smooth components, proving inner iteration bounds (Theorems 3.5 and 3.9). It then proposes an adaptive proximal bundle method (APBM) based on proximal point iterations with this inner solver and proves a total complexity bound for minimizing \(\min_x f(x)\) under Hölder smoothness (Theorem 4.2), which is optimal only in the Lipschitz case \(\alpha=0\).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle-type method for the hybrid convex composite problem \(\min_x f(x)+h(x)\) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\), achieving (up to logs) the optimal rate \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) when \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) has finite diameter \(D\).

This citing paper is related because it (i) uses the same regularized cutting-plane/proximal bundle inner loop (Algorithm 2) and (ii) explicitly discusses the hybrid condition (34), which matches the open problem’s oracle model, noting that Hölder smoothness implies (34) via parameterization. However, the paper’s main optimization algorithm (APBM) is a proximal point method for unconstrained \(\min f\) (no composite \(+h\) term) and is not accelerated; the authors state it is only optimal for \(\alpha=0\) and does not aim for optimal universal/accelerated rates. It does not provide a bundle-type method whose outer iteration complexity matches the stated optimal \(\sqrt{L_f}/\sqrt{\varepsilon}\) term for the smooth part while retaining the \(M_f^2/\varepsilon^2\) term, nor does it address the finite-diameter \(D\) composite setting in the required form.

sources.title: Primal-dual proximal bundle and conditional gradient methods for convex problems
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops a primal-dual proximal bundle framework (PDPB) for convex nonsmooth composite optimization \(\min_x f(x)+h(x)\) under a bounded-subgradient assumption \(\|f'(x)\|\le M\) on \(\mathrm{dom}
 h\), and proves an \(O(M^2 d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) oracle complexity to obtain a primal-dual pair \((\bar x_k,\bar s_k)\) with primal-dual gap \(\hat\phi(\bar x_k)+f^*(\bar s_k)+\hat h^*(-\bar s_k)\le 10\bar\varepsilon\) (Theorem 2.1 with Proposition 2.7). It also proves a duality equivalence between the cutting-plane subroutine PDCP and conditional gradient on the dual proximal subproblem (Theorem 3.1).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle-type method for the hybrid convex composite class with the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\) and bounded domain diameter \(D\), achieving the optimal complexity \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\). This paper analyzes proximal bundle methods mainly under the simpler bounded-subgradient model \(\|f'(x)\|\le M\) (effectively the \(L_f=0\) regime) and obtains the non-accelerated \(O(M^2 d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) rate; it does not incorporate the \(L_f\)-term nor derive the mixed accelerated bound in \(\sqrt{L_f}/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\). The PDCP–conditional-gradient duality and new PDCP/CG variants are potentially useful tools toward acceleration, but no accelerated PB complexity matching the stated optimal bound is proved for the HCCO setting.

sources.title: Stochastic Quadratic Dynamic Programming
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper proposes QCSC (Quadratic Cuts for Strongly Convex optimization), a cutting-plane/bundle-like method that builds models as maxima of quadratic lower cuts, and proves an iteration bound (Theorem 2.1): for minimizing a \(\mu\)-convex \(\tilde f\) over compact convex \(X\) (diameter \(D\)) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M+L\|u-v\|\), QCSC finds an \(\bar\varepsilon\)-optimal solution in at most \[1+\Bigl[1+\tfrac{8(M^2+\bar\varepsilon L)}{\mu\bar\varepsilon}\Bigr]\,\log\Bigl(\tfrac{4\bar t(D)}{3\bar\varepsilon}\Bigr)\] iterations, where \(\bar t(D)=M^2+(\tfrac L2+1)D^2\). It also introduces SQDP, an SDDP variant using quadratic cuts for multistage stochastic programs with strongly convex recourse, and proves almost sure convergence (Theorem 6.4).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle-type method for hybrid convex composite optimization \(\min f+h\) under the hybrid subgradient condition, achieving the optimal complexity \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) (up to logs). This paper does not provide such an accelerated rate. Its deterministic method QCSC targets the \(\mu>0\) strongly convex case and yields a logarithmic-in-\(1/\bar\varepsilon\) complexity of the form \(O\big((1+(M^2+\bar\varepsilon L)/(\mu\bar\varepsilon))\log(1/\bar\varepsilon)\big)\), which is closer to the strongly convex regime bounds in Liang–Monteiro (2023) than to the requested accelerated \(\sqrt{L_f}/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\) term for general \(\mu=0\). It is ‘bundle-type’ (cutting-plane with quadratic cuts) and uses the same hybrid Lipschitz/subgradient growth condition, but it does not address acceleration for the general HCCO class nor match the stated optimal bound in \((L_f,M_f,D,\bar\varepsilon)\).
